input.issue_body:
```
scala> MissingNode.getInstance().asText()
res12: java.lang.String = ""

scala> NullNode.instance.asText()
res13: java.lang.String = null
```

Is it expected that MissingNode returns ""?

input.issue_title: Problem with abstract `Map` types, polymorphic deserialization
input.issue_body:
(note: moved from https://github.com/FasterXML/jackson-core/issues/95)

---

Looks like handling of polymorphic maps fail, when declared `Map` type is abstract, even if polymorphic type information is enabled. Serialization works in such case, but deserialization fails with an error message.

input.pr_title: Implement #644: don't write type id if default impl

If it is, a {@link DatabindException} + * (like 'int' or 'double'). If it is, a {@link DatabindException} * is thrown to indicate this; if not, default value is used - * ({@code 0} for {@code int}, {@code 0.0} for {@code double}, same defaulting as JVM uses). - *

- * Note that this is separate from {@link #FAIL_ON_ABSENT_FOR_PRIMITIVES} added - * in 3.1. + * (0 for 'int', 0.0 for double, same defaulting as what JVM uses). *

* Feature is enabled by default as of Jackson 3.0 (in 2.x it was disabled). */ FAIL_ON_NULL_FOR_PRIMITIVES(true), - /** - * Feature that determines whether absence of a JSON property - * is an error when deserializing into Java primitive types - * (like {@code int} or {@code double}). If it is, a {@link DatabindException} - * is thrown to indicate this; if not, default value is used - * ({@code 0} for {@code int}, {@code 0.0} for {@code double}, same defaulting as JVM uses). - *

- * Note that this is separate from {@link #FAIL_ON_NULL_FOR_PRIMITIVES}: - * that feature controls behavior for explicit JSON {@code null} values, - * while this one controls behavior when a JSON property is entirely absent. - *

- * Feature is disabled by default (added in Jackson 3.1); formerly - * value of {@link #FAIL_ON_NULL_FOR_PRIMITIVES} was used. - * - * @since 3.1 - */ - FAIL_ON_ABSENT_FOR_PRIMITIVES(false), - /** * Feature that determines what happens when type of a polymorphic * value (indicated for example by {@link com.fasterxml.jackson.annotation.JsonTypeInfo}) @@ -194,7 +173,7 @@ public enum DeserializationFeature implements ConfigFeature /** * Feature that determines whether abstract types (abstract classes, interfaces) - * should be ignored when building the type finger prints for polymorphic type + * should be ignored when building the type fingerprints for polymorphic type * deduction using {@link com.fasterxml.jackson.annotation.JsonTypeInfo.Id#DEDUCTION}. * When enabled, non-concrete types are excluded from deduction since they cannot * be instantiated; when disabled, they participate in deduction which may cause diff --git a/src/main/java/tools/jackson/databind/deser/jdk/NumberDeserializers.java b/src/main/java/tools/jackson/databind/deser/jdk/NumberDeserializers.java index a4334a02ab..c55b9dc683 100644 --- a/src/main/java/tools/jackson/databind/deser/jdk/NumberDeserializers.java +++ b/src/main/java/tools/jackson/databind/deser/jdk/NumberDeserializers.java @@ -172,19 +172,6 @@ public final T getNullValue(DeserializationContext ctxt) { return _nullValue; } - // @since 3.1 - @Override - public Object getAbsentValue(DeserializationContext ctxt) { - // [databind#5734]: Absent (missing) JSON fields for primitives controlled - // separately from explicit nulls (FAIL_ON_NULL_FOR_PRIMITIVES) - if (_primitive && ctxt.isEnabled(DeserializationFeature.FAIL_ON_ABSENT_FOR_PRIMITIVES)) { - return ctxt.handleNullForPrimitives(handledType(), ctxt.getParser(), this, -"Cannot map absent (missing) value to type %s (set `DeserializationFeature.FAIL_ON_ABSENT_FOR_PRIMITIVES` to 'false' to allow)", - ClassUtil.nameOf(handledType()));
